title: In A Minute
lyrics:
[verse 1]
I got three laps left to win!
The shells are raining, watch me spin!
Mom yells "homework" in my ear—
I swear I’ll start it… next year!

[chorus]
In a minute, just one more!
In a minute, I’ll be done for sure!
In a minute, wait and see—
I’ll do it… eventually!

[verse 2]
The cheese is cold, the noodles stick,
My kart’s in first—this race is slick!
She says "now," but I say "when?"
I’ll write my name… by next week, then!

[chorus]
In a minute, just one more!
In a minute, I’ll be done for sure!
In a minute, wait and see—
I’ll do it… eventually!

[ending]
The clock says eight, my eyes feel tight…
In a minute… *yawn*… goodnight.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, full musical production, acoustic guitar, bass, and snare, 109 BPM, warm swaying groove, gentle but full, cozy beat, clear warm vocal, strong singalong chorus, every word easy to understand
scene: The kitchen clock ticks 7:17 PM, its red numbers glowing over the half-eaten bowl of mac and cheese; Mom’s voice snaps, *"Your homework’s not going to do itself,"* as the controller vibrates in your palm, the *Mario Kart* race still flashing on the muted TV. "In a minute," you mutter, thumb already mashing the A button for the shortcut through Rainbow Road.
cover_description: A kid in pajamas, controller in hand, mid-air victory jump over a pile of unfinished homework, while a giant cartoon clock looms behind them with arms spinning wildly—one minute to midnight!
